title: A Phone Call with a Friend
summary: Sarah calls her friend Jake to catch up and they discuss their recent activities, weekend plans, and a film they both want to see.

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: Hi Jake! I haven't talked to you in ages. How have you been?

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Sarah! I've been great, thanks. I was actually thinking about calling you. How are things?

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: Pretty good! I started a new job last week, so I've been quite busy. But I'm really enjoying it.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Oh wow, that's exciting! What kind of work is it?

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: I work at a marketing company. I help them write content for social media. It's fun but there's a lot to learn.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: That sounds like a great fit for you. I remember you always loved writing. So what are you up to this weekend?

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: Nothing special yet. I was thinking about going to the cinema. Have you seen that new film everyone is talking about?

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: The one about the astronaut? Yes! I really want to see it. A friend told me it's absolutely brilliant.

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: That's the one! Maybe we could go together on Saturday afternoon?

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: I'd love that. Saturday works perfectly for me. What time were you thinking?

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: How about the three o'clock showing? We could get something to eat afterwards.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Perfect. I know a nice Italian restaurant near the cinema. The pasta there is delicious.

body.speaker: Sarah
body.text: Sounds wonderful! I'll look up the tickets online and send you the details later.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Great. I'm really looking forward to it. It'll be good to catch up properly.

annotations.exampleEn: I haven't seen him in ages — maybe two years!
How are you? — Pretty good, thanks!
I've been quite busy with the new project.
She works for a marketing company that creates ads for brands.
Many businesses now advertise on social media.
That role sounds like a great fit for your skills.

annotations.meaningVi: Bạn đang làm gì / có kế hoạch gì không?
annotations.exampleEn: What are you up to this evening?

annotations.meaningVi: hoàn toàn phù hợp, tiện lắm
annotations.exampleEn: Friday works perfectly for our meeting.

annotations.meaningVi: tra cứu, tìm kiếm thông tin
annotations.exampleEn: I'll look up the train times online.

annotations.meaningVi: trò chuyện đầy đủ để cập nhật tin tức cho nhau
annotations.exampleEn: We need to meet for coffee and catch up properly.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for recent experiences (have + past participle)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì hiện tại hoàn thành (have/has + quá khứ phân từ) diễn tả hành động đã xảy ra trong quá khứ nhưng liên quan đến hiện tại. Ví dụ: 'I haven't talked to you in ages' và 'I've been great'. Cấu trúc này hay dùng với 'just', 'already', 'never', 'ever', và 'in ages'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use have/has + past participle to talk about recent experiences or situations connected to the present. Examples: 'I haven't talked to you in ages', 'I've been quite busy'. Common with words like just, already, never, ever, and in ages.

grammarNotes.title: Was thinking / Were thinking — past continuous for polite suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ tiếp diễn (was/were + V-ing) đôi khi được dùng để diễn tả ý định hoặc đề xuất một cách lịch sự và mềm mại hơn. Ví dụ: 'I was thinking about going to the cinema' nghe lịch sự hơn là 'I want to go to the cinema'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The past continuous (was/were + -ing) can express tentative plans or polite suggestions. Example: 'I was thinking about going to the cinema' is softer and more polite than 'I want to go.' It shows the speaker is open to other ideas.

translationVi:
Sarah: Chào Jake! Tớ chưa nói chuyện với cậu từ rất lâu rồi. Cậu có khoẻ không?
Jake: Sarah! Tớ ổn lắm, cảm ơn cậu. Thực ra tớ cũng đang nghĩ đến chuyện gọi cho cậu. Mọi chuyện thế nào?
Sarah: Khá ổn! Tớ bắt đầu công việc mới tuần trước nên khá bận. Nhưng tớ thực sự thích lắm.
Jake: Ồ tuyệt, thú vị nhỉ! Là công việc gì vậy?
Sarah: Tớ làm ở một công ty tiếp thị. Tớ giúp họ viết nội dung cho mạng xã hội. Vui lắm nhưng có nhiều thứ phải học.
Jake: Nghe có vẻ rất phù hợp với cậu đấy. Tớ nhớ cậu luôn yêu thích việc viết lách. Vậy cuối tuần này cậu có kế hoạch gì không?
Sarah: Chưa có gì đặc biệt. Tớ đang nghĩ đến việc đi xem phim. Cậu đã xem bộ phim mới mà ai cũng đang nói đến chưa?
Jake: Bộ phim về phi hành gia đó à? Rồi! Tớ rất muốn xem. Một người bạn nói với tớ rằng nó cực kỳ tuyệt vời.
Sarah: Đúng cái đó! Có lẽ chúng mình có thể cùng đi vào chiều thứ Bảy không?
Jake: Tớ rất muốn đi. Thứ Bảy hoàn toàn phù hợp với tớ. Cậu nghĩ mấy giờ?
Sarah: Suất chiếu ba giờ nhé? Sau đó chúng mình có thể đi ăn gì đó.
Jake: Tuyệt. Tớ biết một nhà hàng Ý gần rạp phim. Mì pasta ở đó ngon lắm.
Sarah: Nghe tuyệt vời! Tớ sẽ tìm vé online và gửi thông tin cho cậu sau.
Jake: Tốt lắm. Tớ rất mong đến ngày đó. Sẽ tốt khi được trò chuyện đầy đủ với nhau.

questions.prompt: Why does Sarah say she has been quite busy recently?
options: She moved to a new city
She started a new job last week
She has been studying for exams
questions.explanation: Sarah says 'I started a new job last week, so I've been quite busy.'
questions.hint: Something happened at work last week.

questions.explanation: Sarah says 'I work at a marketing company.'
questions.hint: The type of company is related to advertising and promotion.

questions.prompt: What does Sarah do at her job?
options: She writes content for social media
questions.explanation: She says 'I help them write content for social media.'
questions.hint: Her job involves writing for online platforms.

questions.prompt: What does Jake say he remembers about Sarah?
questions.explanation: Jake says 'I remember you always loved writing.'
questions.hint: Jake links her new job to something she enjoyed in the past.

questions.prompt: What does Sarah suggest they do on Saturday?
options: Watch a film at the cinema
questions.explanation: Sarah says 'I was thinking about going to the cinema.'
questions.hint: She mentions a film that everyone is talking about.

questions.prompt: What is the new film about?
questions.explanation: Jake asks 'The one about the astronaut?'
questions.hint: The film's main character works in space.

questions.prompt: What did Jake's friend say about the film?
questions.explanation: Jake says 'A friend told me it's absolutely brilliant.'
questions.hint: It is a very positive comment.

questions.prompt: What time does Sarah suggest for the cinema?
questions.explanation: Sarah says 'How about the three o'clock showing?'
questions.hint: She mentions a specific afternoon time.

questions.prompt: What does Jake suggest doing after the film?
questions.explanation: Jake says 'I know a nice Italian restaurant near the cinema.'
questions.hint: Jake recommends a specific type of cuisine.

questions.prompt: What does Jake say about the food at the restaurant he recommends?
questions.explanation: Jake says 'The pasta there is delicious.'
questions.hint: He praises a specific Italian dish.

questions.prompt: How will Sarah send Jake the ticket details?
options: Later, after looking them up online
questions.explanation: Sarah says 'I'll look up the tickets online and send you the details later.'
questions.hint: She plans to find the information first before sending it.

questions.prompt: How does Jake feel about the Saturday plan?
options: He is looking forward to it
He is worried about the time
questions.explanation: Jake says 'I'm really looking forward to it.'
questions.hint: He uses a phrase that shows anticipation and excitement.

cloze.template: Sarah: Hi Jake! I haven't talked to you in {{b1}}. How have you been? Jake: I've been great, thanks. How are things? Sarah: Pretty good! I started a new {{b2}} last week, so I've been quite busy. But I'm really enjoying it. Jake: Oh wow, that's exciting! What kind of work is it? Sarah: I work at a {{b3}} company. I help them write {{b4}} for social media. Jake: That sounds like a great {{b5}} for you. What are you up to this weekend? Sarah: Nothing special yet. I was thinking about going to the {{b6}}. Have you seen that new film everyone is talking about? Jake: The one about the {{b7}}? Yes! A friend told me it's absolutely brilliant. Sarah: Maybe we could go together on {{b8}} afternoon? Jake: I'd love that. Saturday works {{b9}} for me. Sarah: How about the three o'clock showing? We could get something to eat afterwards. Jake: I know a nice Italian restaurant near the cinema. The {{b10}} there is delicious. Sarah: Sounds wonderful! I'll {{b11}} the tickets online and send you the details later.

blanks.explanation: 'ages' — 'in ages' is a common phrase meaning for a very long time.
'job' — Sarah started a new job last week.
'marketing' — Sarah works at a marketing company.
'content' — she writes content for social media platforms.
'fit' — 'a great fit' means well suited to someone's skills or personality.
'cinema' — Sarah wants to see a new film.
'astronaut' — the film they want to see is about an astronaut.
'Saturday' — they agree to go on Saturday afternoon.
'perfectly' — Jake says Saturday works perfectly for him.
'pasta' — Jake says the pasta at the Italian restaurant is delicious.
'look up' — to look something up means to search for information online or in a reference.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sarah and Jake are making plans to spend time together in person after not seeing each other for a long time. Do you think it is important to maintain friendships even when life gets busy? What can people do to keep in touch with old friends?
